title: Iranian Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Iran
fact: The Iranian Revolution, also known as the Islamic Revolution, culminated in the overthrow of the Pahlavi dynasty in 1979.

title: French Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in France
fact: The French Revolution was a period of political and societal change in France that began with the Estates General of 1789 and ended with the Coup of 18 Brumaire on 9 November 1799.

title: Nanjing Massacre takes place
subtitle: Battle of Nanking
fact: The Nanjing Massacre or the Rape of Nanjing was the mass rape and murder of Chinese civilians, noncombatants, and prisoners of war by the Imperial Japanese Army in Nanjing, the capital of the Republic of China.

title: My Lai Massacre takes place
subtitle: List of massacres in Vietnam
fact: The Mỹ Lai Massacre was a United States war crime committed on 16 March 1968, involving the mass murder of unarmed civilians in Sơn Mỹ village, Quảng Ngãi province, South Vietnam, during the Vietnam War.

title: Arab Spring begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Arab Spring was a series of pro-democracy anti-government protests, uprisings, and armed rebellions that spread across much of the Arab world in the early 2010s.

title: American Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in the United States
fact: The American Revolution (1765–1783) was a political movement in the Thirteen Colonies of Great Britain and the United States which the colonies founded.

title: Cultural Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in China
fact: The Cultural Revolution, formally known as the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution, was a sociopolitical movement in the People's Republic of China (PRC).

title: Boxer Rebellion
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Boxer Rebellion, also known as the Boxer Uprising, Boxer Movement, or Yihetuan Movement, was an anti-foreign, anti-imperialist, and anti-Christian uprising in North China between 1899 and 1901, towards the end of the Qing dynasty, by the Society of Righteous and Harmonious Fists.

title: Cuban Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Cuba
fact: The Cuban Revolution was the military and political movement that overthrew the dictatorship of Fulgencio Batista, who had ruled Cuba from 1952 to 1959.

title: Boston Tea Party
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Boston Tea Party was an act of protest on December 16, 1773 during the American Revolution.

title: White Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Iran
fact: The White Revolution or the Shah and People Revolution was a series of reforms to modernize the Imperial State of Iran launched on 26 January 1963 by the Shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi, and ended with his overthrow in 1979.

title: Mahsa Amini protests
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: Civil unrest and protests against the government of the Islamic Republic of Iran associated with the death in police custody of Mahsa Amini began on 16 September 2022 and carried on into 2023, but by spring 2023, the protests had largely subsided, ultimately leaving the political leadership unchanged and firmly entrenched in power.

title: Glorious Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in England
fact: The Glorious Revolution was the deposition of King James II in November 1688.

title: Stonewall riots
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Stonewall riots were a series of spontaneous riots and demonstrations against a police raid that took place in the early morning of June 28, 1969, at the Stonewall Inn, in the Greenwich Village neighborhood of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

title: Beer Hall Putsch takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Beer Hall Putsch, also known as the Munich Putsch, was a failed coup d'état led by Nazi Party leader Adolf Hitler, General Erich Ludendorff, and other Kampfbund leaders in Munich, Bavaria, on 8–9 November 1923, during the Weimar Republic.

title: Mexican Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Mexico
fact: The Mexican Revolution was an extended sequence of armed regional conflicts in Mexico from 20 November 1910 to 1 December 1920.

title: October Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Russian Republic
fact: The October Revolution, also known as the Great October Socialist Revolution, October coup, Bolshevik coup, Bolshevik Revolution, and occasionally the November Revolution, was the second of two revolutions in Russia in 1917.

title: Kristallnacht
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: Kristallnacht (German pronunciation: [kʁɪsˈtalnaχt] lit. 'crystal night') or the Night of Broken Glass, also called the November pogrom(s) (German: Novemberpogrome, pronounced [noˈvɛm.bɐ.poˌɡʁoːmə] ), was a pogrom against Jews carried out by the Nazi Party's Sturmabteilung (SA) and Schutzstaffel (SS) paramilitary forces along with some participation from the Hitler Youth and German civilians throughout Nazi Germany on 9–10 November 1938.

title: Haitian Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Saint-Domingue
fact: The Haitian Revolution, also known as the Haitian War of Independence, was a successful insurrection by enslaved Africans against French colonial rule in Saint-Domingue, now the sovereign state of Haiti.

title: Boston Massacre takes place
subtitle: American Revolution
fact: The Boston Massacre, known in Great Britain as the Incident on King Street, was a confrontation on March 5, 1770 during the American Revolution in Boston in what was then the colonial-era Province of Massachusetts Bay.

title: Katyn massacre takes place
subtitle: Soviet invasion of Poland
fact: The Katyn massacre was a series of mass executions of Poles carried out by the Soviet Union between April and May 1940.

title: Wounded Knee Massacre takes place
subtitle: Ghost Dance War
fact: The Wounded Knee Massacre, also known as the Battle of Wounded Knee, involved nearly three hundred Lakota people killed by soldiers of the United States Army.

title: Bucha massacre takes place
subtitle: Russian war crimes
fact: The Bucha massacre was the mass murder of Ukrainian civilians and prisoners of war by the Russian Armed Forces during the fight for and occupation of the city of Bucha as part of the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

title: Sabra and Shatila massacre takes place
subtitle: Lebanese Civil War
fact: From 16 to 18 September 1982, between 1,300 and 3,500 civilians—mostly Palestinians and Lebanese Shias—were killed in Beirut's Sabra neighbourhood and the adjacent Shatila refugee camp by the Lebanese Forces, one of the main Christian militias in Lebanon, with the support of the Israel Defense Forces (IDF).

title: Re'im music festival massacre takes place
subtitle: October 7 attacks
fact: On 7 October 2023, the al-Qassam Brigades, the military wing of the Palestinian nationalist Islamist political organization Hamas, initiated a sudden attack on Israel from the Gaza Strip.

title: February Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Russian Empire
fact: The February Revolution, known in Soviet historiography as the February Bourgeois Democratic Revolution, and sometimes as the March Revolution, was the first of two revolutions which took place in Russia in 1917.

title: Revolution of Dignity begins
subtitle: Revolution in Ukraine
fact: The Revolution of Dignity, also known as the Maidan Revolution or the Ukrainian Revolution, took place in Ukraine in February 2014 at the end of the Euromaidan protests.

title: People Power Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Philippines
fact: The People Power Revolution, also known as the EDSA Revolution or the February Revolution, was a series of popular demonstrations in the Philippines, mostly in Metro Manila, from February 22 to 25, 1986.

title: Salt March
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Salt March, also known as the Salt Satyagraha, Dandi March, and the Dandi Satyagraha, was an act of nonviolent civil disobedience in colonial India, led by Mahatma Gandhi.

title: Libyan Civil War begins
subtitle: Revolution in Libya
fact: The Libyan Civil War, also known as the First Libyan Civil War and Libyan Revolution, was an armed conflict fought in 2011 in the North African country of Libya between forces loyal to Colonel Muammar Gaddafi and rebel groups attempting to oust his government.

title: Xinhai Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in China
fact: The 1911 Revolution, also known as the Xinhai Revolution or Hsinhai Revolution, culminated the end of China's last imperial dynasty, the Qing dynasty, and led to the establishment of the Republic of China (ROC).

title: Texas Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Texas Revolution was a rebellion by Anglo-American immigrants as well as Hispanic Texans against the centralist government of Mexico in the Mexican state of Coahuila y Tejas.

title: Neolithic revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Neolithic Revolution, also known as the First Agricultural Revolution, was the wide-scale transition of many human cultures during the Neolithic period from the egalitarian lifestyle of nomadic and semi-nomadic hunter-gatherers to one of agriculture, settlement, establishment of cross-group organisations, population growth and increasing social differentiation.

title: St. Bartholomew's Day massacre takes place
subtitle: French Wars of Religion
fact: The Saint Bartholomew's Day massacre in 1572 was a targeted group of assassinations and a wave of Catholic mob violence directed against the Huguenots during the French Wars of Religion.

title: Euromaidan
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: Euromaidan, or the Maidan Uprising, was a wave of demonstrations and civil unrest in Ukraine, which began on 21 November 2013 with large protests in Maidan Nezalezhnosti in Kyiv.

title: May 68
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: May 68 was a period of widespread protests, strikes, and civil unrest in France that began in May 1968 and became one of the most significant social uprisings in modern European history.

title: Halabja massacre takes place
subtitle: Anfal campaign
fact: The Halabja massacre took place in Iraqi Kurdistan on 16 March 1988, when thousands of Kurds were killed by a large-scale Iraqi chemical attack.

title: George Floyd protests
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The George Floyd protests were a series of protests, riots, and demonstrations against police brutality that began in Minneapolis in the United States on May 26, 2020.

title: Indonesian National Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Indonesia
fact: The Indonesian National Revolution, also known as the Indonesian War of Independence, was an armed conflict and diplomatic struggle between the Republic of Indonesia and the Dutch Empire and an internal social revolution during postwar and postcolonial Indonesia.

title: Coup of 18 Brumaire takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Coup of 18 Brumaire brought Napoleon Bonaparte to power as First Consul of the French First Republic.

title: Occupy Wall Street
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: Occupy Wall Street (OWS) was a left-wing populist and progressive movement against economic inequality, capitalism, corporate greed, big finance, and the influence of money in politics.

title: Velvet Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Czechoslovakia
fact: The Velvet Revolution or Gentle Revolution was a non-violent transition of power in what was then Czechoslovakia, occurring from 17 November to 28 November 1989.

title: Yugoslav coup d'état takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: A coup d'état took place on 27 March 1941 in Belgrade, Kingdom of Yugoslavia, when the regency led by Prince Paul of Yugoslavia was overthrown and King Peter II assumed full monarchical powers.

title: Ludlow Massacre takes place
subtitle: Colorado Coalfield War
fact: The Ludlow Massacre was a mass killing perpetrated by anti-striker militia during the Colorado Coalfield War.

title: Nicaraguan Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Nicaragua
fact: The Nicaraguan Revolution, or Sandinista Revolution was an armed conflict that took place in the Central American nation of Nicaragua between 1961 and 1990.

title: Chinese Communist Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in China
fact: The Chinese Communist Revolution was a social and political revolution in mainland China that began in 1927 and culminated with the proclamation of the People's Republic of China (PRC) in 1949.

title: March on Rome
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The March on Rome was an organized mass demonstration in October 1922 which resulted in Benito Mussolini's National Fascist Party ascending to power in the Kingdom of Italy.

title: Sand Creek massacre takes place
subtitle: Colorado War
fact: The Sand Creek massacre was a massacre of Cheyenne and Arapaho people by the U.S.

title: Saur Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Afghanistan
fact: The Saur Revolution, also known as the April Revolution or the April Coup, was a violent coup d'état and uprising staged on 27 and 28 April 1978 by the People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan (PDPA), which overthrew Afghan president Mohammad Daoud Khan, who had himself taken power in the 1973 Afghan coup d'état and established an autocratic one-party system in the country.

title: Syrian revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Syrian revolution was a series of mass protests and civilian uprisings throughout Syria – with a subsequent violent reaction by the Ba'athist regime – lasting from 2011 to 2024 as part of the greater Arab Spring in the Arab world.

title: Bloody Sunday
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: Bloody Sunday, also known as Red Sunday, was the series of events on Sunday, 22 January [O.S.

title: Kapp Putsch takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Kapp Putsch, also known as the Kapp–Lüttwitz Putsch, was an abortive coup d'état against the German national government in Berlin on 13 March 1920.

title: Orange Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Ukraine
fact: The Orange Revolution was a series of protests that led to political upheaval in Ukraine from late November 2004 to January 2005.

title: Haditha massacre takes place
subtitle: List of massacres in Iraq
fact: The Haditha massacre was a series of killings on November 19, 2005, in which a group of United States Marines killed 25 unarmed Iraqi civilians.

title: Philippine Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Philippines
fact: The Philippine Revolution was a war of independence waged by the revolutionary organization Katipunan against the Spanish Empire from 1896 to 1898.

title: Manila massacre takes place
subtitle: Battle of Manila
fact: The Manila massacre, also called the Rape of Manila, involved atrocities committed against Filipino civilians in the City of Manila, the capital of the Philippines, by Japanese troops during the Battle of Manila which occurred during World War II.

title: May Fourth Movement
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The May Fourth Movement was a Chinese cultural and anti-imperialist political movement which grew out of student protests in Beijing on May 4, 1919.

title: Spartacist uprising
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Spartacist uprising, also known as the January uprising or, more rarely, Bloody Week, was an armed uprising that took place in Berlin from the 5th to 12th of January 1919.

title: Belgian Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in United Kingdom of the Netherlands
fact: The Belgian Revolution was a conflict which led to the secession of the southern provinces from the United Kingdom of the Netherlands and the establishment of an independent Kingdom of Belgium.

title: Yellow Turban Rebellion
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Yellow Turban Rebellion, alternatively translated as the Yellow Scarves Rebellion, was a peasant revolt during the late Eastern Han dynasty of ancient China.

title: Qibya massacre takes place
subtitle: Reprisal operations
fact: The Qibya massacre occurred during Operation Shoshana, an Israeli so-called reprisal operation that occurred in October 1953, when IDF's Unit 101 led by future Israeli prime minister Ariel Sharon attacked the village of Qibya in the West Bank, which was then under Jordanian control, and killed more than 69 Palestinian civilians, two-thirds of whom were women and children.

title: Nika Riots
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Nika riots, Nika revolt or Nika sedition took place against Byzantine emperor Justinian I in Constantinople over the course of a week in 532 AD.

title: Cedar Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Lebanon
fact: The Cedar Revolution, known in Lebanon as the Independence intifada, was a chain of demonstrations in Lebanon triggered by the assassination of former Lebanese Prime Minister Rafic Hariri.

title: Rojava Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Syria
fact: The Rojava Revolution, also known as the Rojava conflict is a political upheaval and military conflict taking place in northern Syria, known among Kurds as Western Kurdistan or Rojava.

title: Hamidian massacres takes place
subtitle: Late Ottoman genocides
fact: The Hamidian massacres also called the Armenian massacres, were massacres of Armenians in the Ottoman Empire in the mid-1890s.

title: Be'eri massacre takes place
subtitle: Stand-off in Be'eri and Ofakim
fact: During the October 7 attacks on Israel, Hamas militants carried out a massacre at Be'eri, a kibbutz near the Gaza Strip.

title: Massacre of Vassy takes place
subtitle: First French War of Religion
fact: The Massacre of Vassy was the murder of Huguenot worshippers and citizens in an armed action by troops of the Duke of Guise, in Wassy, France on 1 March 1562.

title: Abbasid Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Umayyad Caliphate
fact: The Abbasid revolution, was the overthrow of the Umayyad Caliphate (661–750), the second of the four major caliphates in Islamic history, by the third, the Abbasid Caliphate (750–1517).

title: Hama Massacre takes place
subtitle: Islamist revolution in Syria
fact: The Hama massacre occurred in February 1982 when the Syrian Arab Army and the Defense Companies paramilitary force, under the orders of President Hafez al-Assad, besieged the town of Hama for 27 days in order to quell an uprising by the Muslim Brotherhood against the Ba'athist government.

title: Bolivarian Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Venezuela
fact: The Bolivarian Revolution is a social revolution and ongoing political process in Venezuela that was started by Venezuelan President Hugo Chávez, the founder of the Fifth Republic Movement (MVR) and later the United Socialist Party of Venezuela (PSUV), and his successor Nicolás Maduro.

title: August Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Vietnam
fact: The August Revolution, also known as the August General Uprising, was a revolution led by the Việt Minh against the Empire of Vietnam from 13 to 28 August 1945.

title: Damour massacre takes place
subtitle: Lebanese Civil War
fact: The Damour massacre took place on 20 January 1976, during the 1975–1990 Lebanese Civil War.

title: Dersim massacre takes place
subtitle: Kurdish–Turkish conflict
fact: The Dersim massacre, also known as Dersim genocide, was carried out by the Turkish military over the course of three operations in the Dersim Province against Kurdish rebels of Alevi faith, and civilians in 1937 and 1938.

title: Japanese coup d'état in French Indochina takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Japanese coup d'état in French Indochina, known as Meigō Sakusen, was a Japanese operation that took place on 9 March 1945, towards the end of World War II.

title: Baltic Way
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Baltic Way or Baltic Chain was a peaceful political demonstration that occurred on 23 August 1989.

title: Rwandan Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Ruanda-Urundi
fact: The Rwandan Revolution, also known as the Hutu Revolution, Social Revolution, or Wind of Destruction, was a period of ethnic violence in Rwanda from 1959 to 1961 between the Hutu and the Tutsi, two of the three ethnic groups in Rwanda.

title: Gezi Park protests
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: A wave of demonstrations and civil unrest in Turkey began on 28 May 2013, initially to contest the urban development plan for Istanbul's Gezi Park.

title: Chios massacre takes place
subtitle: Massacres during the Greek War of Independence
fact: The Chios massacre was a catastrophe that resulted in the death, enslavement, and flight of about four-fifths of the total population of Greeks on the island of Chios by Ottoman troops during the Greek War of Independence in 1822.

title: Jacquerie
subtitle: Protest, riot, or uprising
fact: The Jacquerie was a popular revolt by peasants that took place in northern France in the early summer of 1358 during the Hundred Years' War.

title: King Michael's Coup takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The 1944 Romanian coup d'état, better known in Romanian historiography as the Act of 23 August, was a coup d'état led by King Michael I of Romania during World War II on 23 August 1944.

title: Vinnytsia massacre takes place
subtitle: Great Purge
fact: The Vinnytsia massacre was the mass execution of over 9,000 people in the Ukrainian town of Vinnytsia by the Soviet secret police NKVD during the Great Purge in 1937–1938, which Nazi Germany discovered during its occupation of Ukraine in 1943.

title: Glorious Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Spain
fact: The Glorious Revolution took place in Spain in 1868, resulting in the deposition of Queen Isabella II.

title: Sétif and Guelma massacre takes place
subtitle: Algeria in World War II
fact: The Sétif and Guelma massacre was a series of massacres by French colonial authorities and pied-noir European settler militias on Algerian civilians in May and June 1945 around the towns of Sétif and Guelma in French Algeria.

title: Foibe massacres takes place
subtitle: World War II
fact: The foibe massacres, or simply the foibe, refers to ethnic cleansing, mass killings and deportations both during and immediately after World War II, mainly committed by Yugoslav Partisans and OZNA in the then-Italian territories of Julian March, Kvarner and Dalmatia, against local Italians and Slavs, primarily members of fascist and collaborationist forces, and civilians opposed to the new Yugoslav authorities, and Italian, German, Croat and Slovene anti-communists against the regime of Josip Broz Tito, presumed to be associated with fascism, Nazism, collaboration with Axis and preventive purge of real, potential or presumed opponents of Titoism.

title: Kfar Aza massacre takes place
subtitle: October 7 attacks
fact: On 7 October 2023, around 250 Hamas and other Palestinian militants attacked Kfar Aza, an Israeli kibbutz about 3 kilometers (1.9 mi) from the border with the Gaza Strip, massacring residents and abducting hostages.

title: Ma'alot massacre takes place
subtitle: Palestinian insurgency in South Lebanon
fact: The Ma'alot massacre was a Palestinian terrorist attack that occurred on 14–15 May 1974 and involved the hostage-taking of 115 Israelis, chiefly school children, which ended in the murder of 25 hostages and six other civilians.

title: May 16 coup takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The May 16 military coup d'état or the May 16 Military Revolution was a military coup d'état in South Korea in 1961, organized and carried out by Park Chung Hee and his allies who formed the Military Revolutionary Committee, nominally led by Army Chief of Staff Chang Do-yong after the latter's acquiescence on the day of the coup.

title: May Coup takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The May Coup was a coup d'état in the Kingdom of Serbia which resulted in the assassination of King Alexander I and his consort, Queen Draga, inside the Stari Dvor in Belgrade on the night of 10–11 June [O.S.

title: Novocherkassk massacre takes place
subtitle: Political repression in the Soviet Union
fact: The Novocherkassk massacre took place on 2 June 1962 in Novocherkassk, Russian SFSR, Soviet Union, when Soviet Army soldiers and MVD Internal Troops, supported by KGB units, fired on unarmed demonstrators protesting economic conditions.

title: Hukbalahap Rebellion begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Hukbalahap rebellion was a rebellion staged in the Philippines by former Hukbalahap or Hukbong Bayan Laban sa Hapon soldiers against the Philippine government.

title: July Putsch takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The July Putsch was a failed coup d'état in Austria against the Fatherland Front government of Engelbert Dollfuss by Austrian Nazis from 25 to 30 July 1934.

title: Nemmersdorf massacre takes place
subtitle: Eastern Front
fact: The Nemmersdorf massacre was a civilian massacre perpetrated by Red Army soldiers in the late stages of World War II.

title: Simele massacre takes place
subtitle: Persecution of Assyrians
fact: The Simele massacre, also known as the Assyrian affair, was a massacre committed by the Kingdom of Iraq under the leadership of Kurdish army general Bakr Sidqi.

title: Constitutionalist Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Brazil
fact: The Constitutionalist Revolution of 1932 was the uprising by the population of the Brazilian state of São Paulo against the Revolution of 1930, when Getúlio Vargas assumed the nation's presidency.

title: May Coup takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The May Coup was a coup d'état carried out in Poland by Marshal Józef Piłsudski from 12 to 14 May 1926.

title: Markale massacres takes place
subtitle: War in Bosnia and Herzegovina
fact: The Markale market shelling or Markale massacres were two separate bombardments, with at least one of them confirmed to have been carried out by the Army of Republika Srpska, targeting civilians during the siege of Sarajevo in the Bosnian War.

title: Champ de Mars Massacre takes place
subtitle: French Revolution
fact: The Champ de Mars massacre took place on 17 July 1791 in Paris at the Champ de Mars against a crowd of republican protesters amid the French Revolution.

title: Sokoto Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution
fact: The Jihad of Usman dan Fodio was a religio-military conflict in present-day Nigeria and Cameroon.

title: Coup of 18 Fructidor takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Coup of 18 Fructidor, Year V, was a seizure of power in France by members of the Directory, then forming the government of the First French Republic, with support from the military.

title: Athenian Revolution begins
subtitle: Revolution in Classical Athens
fact: The Athenian Revolution was a revolt by the people of Athens that overthrew the ruling aristocratic oligarchy, establishing the almost century-long self-governance of Athens in the form of a participatory democracy – open to all free male citizens.

title: Revolution in the Kingdom of Poland begins
subtitle: Revolution in Congress Poland
fact: A major part of the Russian Revolution of 1905 took place in the Russian Partition of Poland and lasted until 1907.

title: Goudi coup takes place
subtitle: Coup d'etat
fact: The Goudi coup was a military coup d'état by a group of military officers that took place on the night of 28 August [O.S.

title: Constitutionalization attempts in Iran begins
subtitle: Revolution in Iran
fact: The Iranian Constitutional Revolution was a short-lived push for democratic rule in the form of a constitutional monarchy within a highly elitist yet decentralized society under the Qajars.
